page: Amazon Web Services c8gd.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by custom 64-bit ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors. It features 16 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, backed by 32.0 GB of system memory. The CPU configuration includes a 36 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 64 KB L1D cache. For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Network connectivity is rated at an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory and processing capabilities. The instance achieved a top-tier Passmark memory score of 3138.89, supported by strong uncached memory bandwidth results of 296.81 GB/sec for large-block reads and 124.13 GB/sec for large-block writes. Geekbench testing shows strong single-core and multi-core performance, alongside strong text processing and bzip3 decompression speeds. Database operations also perform strongly, with Passmark database operations scoring 23981.75 and Redis SET operations processing 35.29 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64-based Graviton architecture provides high resource density and compute performance per watt compared to traditional x86 architectures, especially for workloads that can leverage the bundled 950 GB NVMe local storage without incurring additional EBS costs. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this instance well-suited for compute-intensive workloads, local database hosting, multi-threaded decompression tasks, and high-throughput web serving. However, the lack of AVX or AMX instruction sets and a weak prompt processing score for medium LLM models suggest it is less optimal for heavy machine learning or vector-accelerated workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.4xlarge is a compute-optimized, ARM64-based instance powered by 16 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. It features 32.0 GB of system memory, a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD local instance store, and an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor. Benchmark results show top-tier memory performance with a Passmark memory score of 3138.89, alongside strong uncached memory read bandwidth of 296.81 GB/sec. The instance delivers strong multi-core processing, bzip3 decompression, and database operation performance. With its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and high-speed local storage, the c8gd.4xlarge is highly efficient for compute-intensive applications, high-throughput web serving, and local database workloads, though it lacks x86 vector extensions and is less suited for heavy machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.4xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 32 GB RAM, and a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD for high-performance database and compute workloads.

tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores, high-speed memory bandwidth, and integrated NVMe local storage.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores running at 2.8 GHz
32.0 GB system memory with top-tier Passmark memory performance
Bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD local instance store volume
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Strong performance in database operations and multi-threaded decompression
Optimized for compute-heavy workloads, web serving, and local databases
